# terrible_cisco_aci_aci_igmp_interface_policy (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `allow_v3_asm` (Boolean) Enable the Allow v3 ASM option. The APIC defaults to False when unset during creation. If this parameter is set, fast_leave and report_link_local_groups must also be set.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) The description of the IGMP Interface Policy.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `fast_leave` (Boolean) Enable the Fast Leave option. The APIC defaults to False when unset during creation. If this parameter is set, allow_v3_asm and report_link_local_groups must also be set.
- `group_timeout` (Number) The IGMP group timeout in seconds. The APIC defaults to 260 when unset during creation.
- `igmp_version` (String) The IGMP version to run. The APIC defaults to v2 when unset during creation.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `last_member_count` (Number) The last member query count. The APIC defaults to 2 when unset during creation.
- `last_member_response` (Number) The last member response time in seconds. The APIC defaults to 1 when unset during creation.
- `name` (String) The name of the IGMP Interface Policy.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `querier_timeout` (Number) The querier timeout in seconds. The APIC defaults to 255 when unset during creation.
- `query_interval` (Number) The IGMP query interval in seconds. The APIC defaults to 125 when unset during creation.
- `query_response_interval` (Number) The IGMP query response interval in seconds. The APIC defaults to 10 when unset during creation.
- `report_link_local_groups` (Boolean) Enable the Report Link Local Groups option. The APIC defaults to False when unset during creation. If this parameter is set, allow_v3_asm and fast_leave must also be set.
- `robustness_variable` (Number) The robustness factor. The APIC defaults to 2 when unset during creation.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `startup_query_count` (Number) The Startup Query Count. The APIC defaults to 2 when unset during creation.
- `startup_query_interval` (Number) The startup query interval in seconds. The APIC defaults to 31 when unset during creation.
- `state` (String) Use `present` or `absent` for adding or removing. Use `query` for listing an object or multiple objects.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tenant` (String) The tenant to build the IGMP Interface Policy under.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `current` (String) The existing configuration from the APIC after the module has finished
- `error` (String) The error information as returned from the APIC
- `filter_string` (String) The filter string used for the request
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `method` (String) The HTTP method used for the request to the APIC
- `previous` (String) The original configuration from the APIC before the module has started
- `proposed` (String) The assembled configuration from the user-provided parameters
- `raw` (String) The raw output returned by the APIC REST API (xml or json)
- `response` (String) The HTTP response from the APIC
- `sent` (String) The actual/minimal configuration pushed to the APIC
- `status` (Number) The HTTP status from the APIC
- `url` (String) The HTTP url used for the request to the APIC
